skill.name: ffuf-claude-skill
skill.description: Guidance for planning and discussing web fuzzing workflows with ffuf.
skill.summary: Plan web fuzzing tasks with ffuf-focused guidance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static hardcoded URL findings are true positives for external links, but they point to GitHub source references and are not executable network calls. The weak cryptographic algorithm findings at SKILL.md lines 3 and 20 are false positives from descriptive ffuf text, with no cryptographic implementation present. No prompt injection, scripts, command execution, or data exfiltration evidence was found.

low_findings.title: External Source Repository Links
low_findings.description: SKILL.md contains hardcoded GitHub URLs used as source attribution and further reading. This is a low risk because the links are static documentation references and no automatic network request is defined.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are directly visible in the skill file and only appear as metadata and Markdown documentation links. There is no script, fetch call, or instruction that sends user data to those URLs.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Detection
low_findings.description: The static weak cryptographic algorithm findings on SKILL.md lines 3 and 20 do not indicate real cryptographic behavior. The lines describe web fuzzing with ffuf and do not include hashing, encryption, or algorithm selection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both locations contain plain descriptive text about ffuf. No cryptographic API, weak algorithm name, or executable code is present in the reviewed file.

content.value_statement: Web fuzzing can be hard to scope and document clearly. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code guide ffuf-focused reconnaissance planning and review.
actual_capabilities: Provides high-level guidance for web fuzzing with ffuf.
Helps decide when ffuf is relevant for web reconnaissance.
Frames ffuf usage around patterns and planning rather than automation.
Points users to the upstream source repository for more detail.
Supports security testing discussions in Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
limitations: Does not include executable scripts, wrappers, or automation files.
Does not provide bundled wordlists, payloads, or scan profiles.
Does not verify target authorization or legal scope.
Provides minimal guidance and depends on user-provided context.

use_cases.title: Scope a Reconnaissance Task
use_cases.description: Prepare a focused ffuf plan for authorized content discovery against a web application.

use_cases.title: Review Fuzzing Options
use_cases.description: Compare where ffuf fits in a broader web testing workflow before running tools.

use_cases.title: Document Test Intent
use_cases.description: Create clear notes about why web fuzzing is needed and what boundaries apply.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Basic ffuf Test
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for an authorized target
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me plan a basic ffuf web fuzzing task for an authorized application. Include goals, scope, and safe assumptions.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Fuzzing Targets
prompt_templates.prompt: Given this authorized web application context, identify where ffuf may help with content discovery and where it may be unnecessary.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Testing Notes
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn my ffuf testing plan into concise engagement notes with target scope, expected results, and risk controls.

prompt_templates.title: Review an Advanced Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced validation of a security workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this ffuf-based reconnaissance workflow for clarity, authorization boundaries, rate limits, and evidence collection.

output_examples.input: I need to fuzz an authorized staging site.
output_examples.output: A scoped testing plan with objectives, target boundaries, rate considerations, and expected discovery categories.

output_examples.input: I have several endpoints and need to decide where ffuf helps.
output_examples.output: A prioritized list of endpoints where content discovery is useful, plus cases where fuzzing adds little value.

output_examples.input: I need to document my ffuf approach for a client report.
output_examples.output: Professional testing notes that explain intent, scope, controls, and how findings should be recorded.

best_practices: Use this skill only for systems where testing is authorized.
Define scope, rate limits, and evidence handling before planning fuzzing.
Record assumptions and keep results tied to specific authorized targets.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to target systems without permission.
Do not treat the skill as an automated scanner or exploit tool.
Do not omit rate limits, scope limits, or legal authorization details.

faq.question: Does this skill run ffuf automatically?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed skill provides guidance and patterns only. It does not include executable automation.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found only low risk documentation links and false positive crypto detections.

faq.question: What tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it include wordlists?
faq.answer: No. The skill file does not include wordlists, payload sets, or scan profiles.

faq.question: Can it verify authorization for testing?
faq.answer: No. Users must confirm authorization and scope before applying any security testing guidance.

faq.question: Why is the risk level low?
faq.answer: The file contains external GitHub links, but no executable code, command execution, or data exfiltration behavior.
